results.title: ATM Pulled Out Of Kiosk In Daring Balasore Heist, Police Launch Investigation in Odisha | News187
results.description: | A daring ATM theft has been reported from Odisha's Balasore, where unidentified miscreants allegedly uprooted an entire ATM from its kiosk before fleeing the scene. The incident has prompted a police investigation, with officials examining CCTV footage and gathering forensic evidence to identify those involved. The shocking heist has raised fresh concerns over ATM security and law enforcement measures in the region. Watch the latest updates, police action, and developments from Balasore. n18oc_breaking-news News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

results.description: Emergency services were called to a burst water pipe, which flooded properties along Knaresborough Road in Harrogate.

results.title: Supriya Sule Denies Rumours Of Contesting From Two Constituencies, Calls Social Media Claim 'Wrong Information'
results.description: Nationalist Congress Party - Sharadchandra Pawar (NCP-SP) Baramati MP Supriya Sule has dismissed social media claims that she will contest from two constituencies in the upcoming elections. Responding to a post on X (formerly Twitter) that shared the claim, Sule wrote, "You are spreading wrong information about me"

results.title: Fire services busts illegal fuel station following viral photo of ImmD minibus
results.description: An illicit refueling operation in Tuen Mun has been dismantled by the Fire Services Department following the wide circulation of a viral photograph depicting an outsourced Immigration Department (ImmD) minibus purchasing illegal diesel.

results.title: Delayed parenthood leading to declining birth rate: Expert
results.description: Delayed parenthood, changing priorities and financial pressures are contributing to Malaysia's declining birth rate, says a family studies expert.

results.title: Navy recovers 43,000 litres illegally refined petroleum products in Rivers
results.description: The Nigerian Navy has dismantled another cluster of illegal refining sites and discovered 43,000 litres of suspected illegally refined petroleum products in Rivers. This is contained in an operational report by the Director of Naval Information, Capt. Abiodun Folorunsho, made available to the News Agency of Nigeria (NAN) Abuja.
